questions.question: In supported living, what is typically true of the people who receive support?
options: They stay only for short respite breaks
They hold their own tenancy and choose the support they receive
They receive care but make none of their own choices
They live in a hospital ward

questions.question: What does 'domiciliary care' refer to?
options: Care given only in a nursing home
Supporting people in their own homes, often travelling between several people in a shift
Round-the-clock care in a residential home
Daytime activities at a community centre

questions.question: Which of these best describes extra care housing?
options: A residential home with staff on site around the clock
Having your own flat with care and support available on site if you need it

questions.question: Besides supporting the individual, what is one benefit of day services?
options: They replace the need for any home care
They remove the need for a care plan
They give unpaid family carers a welcome break

questions.question: What is the title of Care Certificate Standard 1?
What is a Personal Development Plan (PDP)?
options: A moving and handling risk assessment
A written plan, usually reviewed each year, setting out what you need to learn and how

questions.question: The CQC checks whether care services are which of the following?
options: Safe, effective, caring, responsive and well-led

questions.question: What does the 'duty of candour', one of the CQC fundamental standards, mean?
options: Completing the Care Certificate on time
Working only within your job description
Keeping all mistakes secret from families
Being open and honest, especially when something goes wrong

questions.question: Under the Equality Act, what is it unlawful to do?
options: Offer someone a choice of what to eat
Treat someone worse because of things like their age, disability, race, religion or sexual orientation
Involve someone in decisions about their own care
Ask someone the name they prefer to be called

questions.question: What does promoting a person's independence mean in practice?
options: Doing everything for the person to save time
Doing things with a person rather than for them, so they keep the skills and confidence they already have
Making all decisions on the person's behalf
Discouraging the person from taking any risks at all

questions.question: Which of these is an example of respecting a person's dignity during personal care?
options: Rushing them so the visit finishes sooner
Discussing their care with your own family
Knocking before you enter and keeping them covered

questions.question: If you are asked to carry out a task you have not been trained for, what should you do?
options: Ask another new starter to do it instead
Do it quickly to save time
Stop, ask, and not carry it out until you are trained
Guess how it should be done

questions.question: Which law, alongside the UK General Data Protection Regulation, governs how personal information must be handled?
options: The Health and Safety at Work Act

questions.question: Confidentiality is important, but if you believe someone is at risk of harm or abuse, what should you do?
options: Pass your concern on straight away
Keep the information secret to protect their trust
Wait until your next supervision to mention it

questions.question: In supported living, people hold their own tenancy and choose the support they receive.
Confidentiality is absolute, so you must never share information even if you believe someone is at risk of harm.
Health and safety at work is a shared responsibility between the employer and the worker.
To save time, it is acceptable to carry out a moving and handling task you have not been trained to do.
Which of the following are among the CQC's five key questions about a service? (Select all that apply)
In which of the following settings is adult social care delivered? (Select all that apply)
Which of the following are among the CQC fundamental standards? (Select all that apply)
Which of the following are part of your personal development under Care Certificate Standard 2? (Select all that apply)
Promoting independence means doing things ___ a person rather than ___ them.
Care Certificate Standard 2 is about your personal ___.
The five key questions the CQC asks are whether a service is safe, effective, caring, responsive and well-___.
